reasoning: The document contains a total of 6 major defects (2 LLM fidelity defects and 4 programmatic defects). Issues include flattened nested lists across page boundaries, missing/broken images, table captions coded as header rows, displaced hyperlinks, and mislabeled table headers. Because the total number of major defects strictly exceeds the threshold of 5, the document must be routed to REJECT.

concerns.analysis: While most lists in the document are successfully captured, there is a consistent issue with nested lists spanning page boundaries. In the multiple-choice section, the sub-items (a, b, c, d) are supposed to be nested as children of the numbered question. However, when the question spans a page break, the sub-items are flattened to the same level as the parent question. For example, in Question 3 (pages 58-59), 'a. iron nail' is properly nested, but 'b', 'c', and 'd' are extracted as sibling item
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Question 3: '3. Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of a mineral? 
 a. iron nail 
 [page break] 
 b. penny 
 c. fingernail 
 d. all of the above'
concerns.output_quote: "items": [ { "text": "Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of a mineral?", "children": [ { "text": "a. iron nail" } ] }, { "text": "b. penny" }, { "text": "c. fingernail" }, { "text": "d. all of the above" } ]

concerns.analysis: [Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.00, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] Several meaningful images from the rock identification guide are missing or broken in the HTML. On page 54, images 5 and 6 are rendered as text placeholders (`[Image: A collection of loose...]`) rather than actual `` tags. Additionally, images 9 and 10 (along with their captions 'nesoil.com' and 'www.galleries.com') are completely missing from the HTML output.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 3 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=3. Examples: p28: "MOHS SCALE OF HARDNESS"; p35: "Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests"; p37: "Complete Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 16 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=16, examples=['http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll', 'http://www.project2061.org/publications/bsl/online/bointro.h', 'http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Unit Title: Rocks and Minerals"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.
Text consistently appears as first or last element on 2/61 pages — likely a running header/footer
header_footer_count=58. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 5 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=5. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Ensure nested list items are properly nested inside their parent `<ol>` or `<ul>` when spanning page breaks
Restore missing images 5, 6, 9, and 10 on page 54, replacing text placeholders with `` tags
Use `<caption>` elements for table titles instead of `<tr><th>` spanning all columns
Integrate displaced standalone links directly into their respective paragraphs
Fix table markup to ensure `<th>` elements are only used for header cells, not data cells
Address static form field conversion appropriately for accessibility
